Status & Setting Conference On Defendant Lawrence Goldfarb'S Motion To Disqualify David Given And The Law Firm Of Phillips, Erlewine & Given, Llp, And To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Feb-09-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on Defendant & Cross-Defendant Lawrence R. Goldfarb's and Cross-Defendant LRG Capital Group, LLC's Motion to Disqualify and to Stay Proceedings, and for Status & Setting Conference, having been continued from January 5, 2012.
Judge: James J. McBride; Clerk: Kevin R. Dougherty; CSR: Not Reported.

Nicholas A. Carlin, Esq. appeared for defendant & cross-complainant Pyramid Interactive, Inc. and Respondent Christopher Smith.
Rod O. Divelbiss appeared for defendant & cross-defendant Lawrence R. Goldfarb, and cross-defendant LRG Capital Group, LLC.
Andrew Castricone appeared for plaintiff Christopher Smith.

After considering the pleadings & papers, and hearing argument by counsel, and good cause appearing therefor, the Court finds that there is inadequate evidence to suppor the motion to disqualify the Law Offices of Phillips, Erlewine & Given, LLP or David M. Given as counsel of record.

Accordingly, the motion to disqualify and to stay proceedings is DENIED. The Status & Setting Conference is taken off calendar as being MOOT.

Nicholas A. Carlin, attorney for defendant & cross-complainant Pyramid Interactive, Inc. and respondent Christopher Smith shall prepare the Order after Hearing.

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on Defendants Lawrence Goldfarb, a/k/a Larry Goldfarb's motion to disqualify David Given and the law firm of Phillips, Erlewine & Given, LLP and to stay the proceedings.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Not Reported.

Nicholas Carlin, Jerome Fiskin and Andrew Castrilone appeared for plaintiff Christopher Smith, who was not present at today's hearing.
Robert Divelbiss appeared for defendant and moving party Lawrence Goldfarb, a/k/a Larry Goldfarb, who was not present at today's hearing.

After hearing oral argument by counsel, and good appearing therfor, the Court Orders that the parties may take the depositions of Larry Goldberg, David Given and John O'Neil with a Referee present. Counsel shall meet and & confer as to the scope of the questioning at the depositions, and to select a Referee. Each deposition shall be no longer than three hours in length, but there shall not be any carry over if one of the depositions goes less than three hours. (i.e. one deposition last two hours, one of the other depositions cannot last for four hours). Party taking the deposition shall bear the costs of the deposition.

Matter is CONTINUED to February 9, 2012 @ 9:00 a.m. in Department 220 for Status & Setting on this present motion. No addditional filings shall be permitted for the hearing on February 9th. Lastly, any and all objections from any party to late filings for today's hearing are DENIED.

Counsel shall submit a proposed Order After Hearing to the Court for review no later than 5:00 p.m. on Tuesday, January 10, 2012.
